network.overall_strategy: This network uses a multi-environment prebiotic scenario grounded in the literature: hydrothermal CO2 fixation generates shared carbon feedstocks and proto-energetic phosphoryl donors; surface photochemistry and evaporitic chemistry produce adenine, sugar precursors, ribose or ribose surrogates, adenosine, and phosphorylated adenine nucleotides; biochemical/prebiotic assembly then connects these validated intermediates to ADP and finally ATP through the experimentally demonstrated acetyl-phosphate/Fe3+-mediated phosphorylation of ADP. The design intentionally includes parallel routes to adenine, ribose, adenosine, AMP, ADP, and ATP to reflect the integration challenge and the currently fragmented but experimentally supported state of prebiotic ATP chemistry.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal CO2 reduction to formate

conditions.other: alkaline hydrothermal vent conditions, H2-rich, pH 9-11

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-surface catalytic hydrogenation of CO2 to reduced C1 species on Fe-S phases under alkaline vent conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Preiner et al. (2020) demonstrated CO2 fixation with H2 on greigite, magnetite, and related hydrothermal minerals at 100°C under alkaline conditions, giving substantial formate yields; this provides a hydrothermal entry point into reduced carbon chemistry.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal reduction of CO2-derived carbon to formaldehyde

conditions.catalyst: Ni-doped mackinawite / magnetite-like hydrothermal mineral surfaces
conditions.other: alkaline vent reactor analogue with proton gradient context

reactions.mechanism: Hydrogen-driven reduction of CO2 at iron-sulfur mineral interfaces produces C1 oxygenates including formaldehyde.
reactions.reasoning: Herschy et al. (2014) detected formaldehyde from H2 + CO2 in alkaline vent simulation reactors using Ni-doped mackinawite catalysts. This justifies formaldehyde as a hydrothermal feedstock exported to surface settings.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal proto-energetic synthesis of acetyl phosphate

conditions.other: speculative connecting step consistent with acetyl-phosphate-first vent scenarios

reactions.mechanism: Reduced C1/C2 carbon and phosphate are coupled at Fe-rich hydrothermal interfaces to generate an activated acyl phosphate donor.
reactions.reasoning: Lane and Martin's hydrothermal framework and the review identify acetyl phosphate as a plausible proto-ATP intermediate, but no single direct geochemical synthesis under the exact full sequence is established; this step is explicitly speculative yet necessary to connect vent carbon fixation to the experimentally validated ATP-forming step of Pinna et al. (2022).

reactions.name: Surface photoredox synthesis of glycolaldehyde from HCN

conditions.catalyst: UV-driven Cu(I)/Cu(II) cyanide photoredox network on mineral-bearing surface settings
conditions.other: UV irradiation in cyanosulfidic surface environment

reactions.mechanism: HCN undergoes UV-driven homologation/reduction in a catalytic photoredox network to produce C2 sugar precursors.
reactions.reasoning: Ritson & Sutherland (2012) demonstrated UV-driven formation of glycolaldehyde from HCN via Cu-cyanide photoredox chemistry, and Patel et al. (2015) integrated such sugar-fragment chemistry into a broader cyanosulfidic network. Surface mineral agents are included to satisfy the network schema, though the key experimental catalyst is the Cu cyanide photoredox system.

reactions.name: Surface photoredox synthesis of glyceraldehyde from HCN

conditions.catalyst: UV-driven Cu(I)/Cu(II) cyanide photoredox network on mineral-bearing surface settings
conditions.other: UV irradiation in cyanosulfidic surface environment

reactions.mechanism: Continued HCN photoredox homologation produces C3 sugar aldehydes feeding nucleotide precursor synthesis.
reactions.reasoning: Ritson & Sutherland (2012) showed photoredox formation of glyceraldehyde alongside glycolaldehyde from HCN chemistry; Patel et al. (2015) support integration into a cyanosulfidic prebiotic network. Mineral agents are listed to fit required surface reaction metadata.

reactions.name: Borate-stabilized formose entry to ribose

conditions.other: alkaline evaporitic pool; borate stabilization and pentose-guiding conditions

reactions.mechanism: Formose-like sugar assembly from formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde is redirected by borate complexation to stabilize ribose and related pentoses.
reactions.reasoning: Ricardo et al. (2004) showed borate stabilizes ribose; Kim et al. (2011) further demonstrated borate/molybdate-guided conversion of formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde mixtures toward pentoses including ribose. The reaction remains environmentally plausible but subject to the ribose-formation limitations emphasized by Krishnamurthy et al. (2025).

reactions.name: HCN oligomerization to DAMN

conditions.catalyst: concentration/eutectic and base-catalyzed oligomerization on mineral-rich surfaces
conditions.other: requires locally concentrated HCN; eutectic freezing or evaporation plausible

reactions.mechanism: HCN undergoes sequential oligomerization to the tetramer diaminomaleonitrile, the key bottleneck in purine assembly.
reactions.reasoning: Sanchez, Ferris & Orgel (1967) identified DAMN as the critical HCN tetramer intermediate, and Sandström et al. (2025) confirmed DAMN as a necessary intermediate in liquid HCN microkinetic models. The mineral listing reflects concentrating/stabilizing surface contexts rather than a unique required catalyst.

reactions.name: UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN
reactions.mechanism: Photochemical rearrangement of DAMN furnishes the imidazole nitrile intermediate AICN en route to adenine.
reactions.reasoning: The review identifies near-quantitative UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN as the accepted path toward adenine, following the mechanistic framework developed after Oró and Sanchez/Ferris/Orgel and reinforced by Sandström et al. (2025).

reactions.name: Adenine formation from AICN and HCN

conditions.catalyst: thermal concentration of ammonium cyanide-derived HCN system

reactions.mechanism: AICN incorporates a fifth HCN equivalent and cyclizes to the purine skeleton, giving adenine.
reactions.reasoning: Oró (1961) established adenine formation from concentrated ammonium cyanide, and the accepted mechanistic sequence summarized in the review routes this through AICN plus a fifth HCN equivalent to adenine.

reactions.name: Formamide mineral-catalyzed adenine synthesis
reactions.mechanism: Thermal condensation/rearrangement of formamide on mineral surfaces generates purine nucleobases including adenine.
reactions.reasoning: Saladino et al. (2003) showed TiO2-catalyzed nucleobase synthesis from formamide, and Saladino et al. (2004) extended this to montmorillonite-catalyzed reactions. The review highlights formamide as a robust alternative to concentrated HCN for adenine production.

reactions.name: 2-Aminooxazole formation from glycolaldehyde and cyanamide
reactions.mechanism: Condensation of glycolaldehyde with cyanamide yields the aminooxazole heterocycle central to Sutherland-type nucleotide precursor assembly.
reactions.reasoning: Anastasi et al. (2006) demonstrated formation of 2-aminooxazole from glycolaldehyde and cyanamide as the entry point to the Sutherland sugar-scaffold pathway.

reactions.name: Ribo-aminooxazoline formation from 2-aminooxazole and glyceraldehyde

conditions.other: reported 44% ribo-aminooxazoline yield with accompanying arabino isomer

reactions.mechanism: Aminooxazole captures glyceraldehyde to form a ribose-containing heterocyclic adduct that bypasses free ribose instability.
reactions.reasoning: Anastasi et al. (2006) reported ribo-aminooxazoline formation in 44% yield, providing the sugar-scaffold strategy emphasized by Powner, Gerland & Sutherland (2009).

reactions.name: Selective adenosine formation from adenine and ribose

conditions.other: mineral-mediated direct ribosylation with N9 selectivity

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-assisted glycosylation couples adenine to ribose with preference for N9 ribosylation, furnishing adenosine.
reactions.reasoning: The 2022 Nature Synthesis study highlighted in the review showed Ti-containing minerals catalyze direct ribosylation of adenine with significant N9 selectivity (~14% yield, 13:1 N9:N6). This is the most directly relevant mineral-mediated route to adenosine.

reactions.name: Borate-directed phosphorylation of adenosine to 5'-AMP

conditions.other: formamide medium; borate blocks 2' and 3' OH groups to enforce 5' selectivity

reactions.mechanism: Borate complexes the vicinal cis-diols of ribose, suppressing alternative phosphorylation sites and directing phosphoester formation to the 5' position.
reactions.reasoning: Kim et al. (2016) showed borate-directed regioselective phosphorylation of adenosine in formamide to exclusively 5'-AMP, addressing a major prebiotic selectivity problem.

reactions.name: DAP-mediated phosphorylation of adenosine to AMP

conditions.other: pH 5.5-10; low-water paste or aqueous settings possible

reactions.mechanism: DAP acts as a condensed phosphoryl donor, activating nucleoside hydroxyls under mild aqueous conditions to produce monophosphates.
reactions.reasoning: Gibard et al. (2018) demonstrated that DAP phosphorylates nucleosides under mild aqueous prebiotic conditions, and Cruz et al. (2023) improved understanding of regioselective nucleoside phosphorylation by DAP.

reactions.name: Trimetaphosphate-mediated conversion of AMP to ADP
reactions.mechanism: Reactive cyclic polyphosphate transfers additional phosphoryl units to adenylate during dehydrating cycles, producing higher phosphorylated adenine nucleotides including ADP.
reactions.reasoning: Yamagata et al. (1991) and Yamagata & Inomata, as summarized in the review, demonstrated trimetaphosphate-driven phosphorylation of adenosine under wet-dry cycling with Ni(II), yielding ATP among products; representing ADP as an intermediate in this polyphosphate ladder is a literature-consistent network abstraction.

reactions.name: Acetyl phosphate and Fe3+-mediated phosphorylation of ADP to ATP
reactions.mechanism: Fe3+ forms a macrochelate involving adenine N7, the acetyl phosphate phosphate, and the ADP beta-phosphate, enabling selective terminal phosphorylation to ATP.
reactions.reasoning: Pinna et al. (2022) provided the first direct plausible prebiotic ATP synthesis: acetyl phosphate with Fe3+ converts ADP to ATP in water at 30°C and pH 5.5-6 in ~15-20% yield, and selectively favors the adenine nucleotide.

reactions.name: One-pot trimetaphosphate phosphorylation of adenosine to ATP

conditions.other: volcanic polyphosphate conditions; reported 13% 5'-ATP among products

reactions.mechanism: Cyclic polyphosphate delivers multiple phosphoryl groups to adenosine under dehydrating conditions, yielding ATP directly among a mixture of adenylate products.
reactions.reasoning: As summarized in the review from Yamagata et al. (1991) and Yamagata & Inomata, trimetaphosphate with Ni(II) under wet-dry cycling phosphorylated adenosine to give 5'-ATP directly (~13%) together with other phosphorylated products.

pathways.name: HCN adenine + borate ribose + mineral ribosylation + borate AMP + cTMP ADP + AcP ATP
pathways.description: A multi-environment route beginning with hydrothermal formaldehyde production and surface HCN oligomerization to adenine, converging with borate-stabilized ribose, then proceeding through adenosine, 5'-AMP, ADP, and ATP via acetyl phosphate.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine + borate ribose + mineral ribosylation + borate AMP + AcP ATP
pathways.description: Adenine is produced from neat formamide on mineral surfaces, then joined to borate-stabilized ribose and advanced through AMP and ADP to ATP.

pathways.name: HCN adenine + borate ribose + mineral ribosylation + DAP AMP + AcP ATP
pathways.description: Uses the HCN oligomerization route to adenine, but DAP as the monophosphorylation entry before conversion to ADP and ATP.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine + borate ribose + mineral ribosylation + DAP AMP + AcP ATP
pathways.description: Combines formamide mineral catalysis for adenine formation with DAP phosphorylation and the Pinna acetyl-phosphate ATP step.

pathways.name: HCN adenine + borate ribose + direct cTMP ATP
pathways.description: A shorter surface-heavy route in which adenosine formed from adenine and ribose is phosphorylated directly to ATP by trimetaphosphate chemistry.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine + borate ribose + direct cTMP ATP
pathways.description: Adenine from formamide converges with borate-stabilized ribose, followed by direct polyphosphate-based ATP formation from adenosine.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal formaldehyde to ribose branch feeding HCN adenine branch then ATP
pathways.description: Explicitly emphasizes hydrothermal-to-surface transport of formaldehyde before coupling with the classical HCN purine route and biochemical ATP completion.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal formate to acetyl phosphate branch merged with surface adenosine branch
pathways.description: Vent chemistry generates the energetic donor acetyl phosphate in parallel while surface chemistry generates adenosine and ADP; both streams converge in ATP formation.

pathways.name: HCN sugar-fragment scaffold branch connected to ATP through adenosine convergence
pathways.description: Uses the experimentally supported Sutherland sugar-fragment scaffold chemistry as a parallel validated precursor branch that converges on the same sugar inventory feeding adenosine assembly and ATP synthesis.

pathways.name: Parallel adenine convergence route with direct cTMP ATP finish
pathways.description: Both adenine-forming routes converge on the same adenine hub, which then enters ribose coupling and direct trimetaphosphate ATP production from adenosine.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is a chemically connected ATP route and captures the literature-supported terminal ADP-to-ATP step very well. The hydrothermal-to-surface-to-biochemical progression is strong, and adenine formation via DAMN/AICN is well aligned with Oró/Sanchez/Ferris/Orgel literature. Major weaknesses are the speculative hydrothermal acetyl phosphate step from formate + phosphate, the weakly supported direct adenine + ribose glycosylation to adenosine, and the overstated confidence in free-ribose formose chemistry given current doubts about linear ribose production under mild conditions.
suggestions: Replace free-ribose-dependent nucleoside assembly with a better-supported purine nucleoside route such as the Benner cyclic phosphate or Carell-type selective purine nucleoside chemistry.
Justify acetyl phosphate formation with a more plausible acetate/thioester-based or vent-energy-coupled route rather than direct formate + phosphate.
Specify the known cTMP phosphorylation conditions more closely, including Ni(II) and wet-dry cycling where relevant.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Using formamide for adenine improves plausibility relative to the HCN-only branch because it bypasses the HCN concentration problem and aligns well with Saladino et al. However, the route still depends on the weakest part of the network: ribose generation plus direct adenine-ribose condensation. The final ATP-forming step is excellent and literature-aligned.
suggestions: Carry the formamide branch into a more selective purine nucleoside assembly route rather than direct adenine + ribose condensation.
Clarify whether borate-directed AMP formation is from orthophosphate alone or from phosphate + pyrophosphate/formamide conditions as in the literature.
Strengthen the hydrothermal branch by generating acetate/acetyl phosphate through more established vent chemistry.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is one of the stronger routes because DAP-mediated adenosine phosphorylation is better supported than the orthophosphate-only AMP step. The HCN adenine chemistry is solid, and the final AcP/Fe3+ ATP step is excellent. The core weakness remains the direct adenosine formation from adenine + ribose and the uncertain free-ribose branch.
suggestions: Retain DAP phosphorylation but revise nucleoside formation to a literature-backed selective purine ribosylation route.
If keeping free ribose, explicitly acknowledge low yield/mixed sugar output and lower confidence.
Use experimentally grounded cTMP-to-ADP conditions and catalysts.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is likely the best of the ADP-mediated routes. Formamide-to-adenine and DAP-mediated phosphorylation are both strongly grounded in literature, and the ADP-to-ATP conversion via acetyl phosphate and Fe3+ is the best-supported final step in the whole network. It still falls short of approval because adenosine formation from adenine + ribose is insufficiently justified and free-ribose chemistry remains a major bottleneck.
suggestions: Swap direct ribosylation for Ti-mineral-catalyzed ribosylation or cyclic-phosphate-directed adenosine formation from the literature.
Narrow the hydrothermal branch to species actually demonstrated in vent simulations.
Differentiate clearly between a speculative network bridge and experimentally validated chemistry.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The direct adenosine-to-ATP route is attractive topologically but chemically weaker than the ADP-to-ATP branch. cTMP phosphorylation of adenosine can produce ATP under some reported conditions, but selectivity is mixed and the route is less robust than the AcP/Fe3+ ADP route emphasized in the literature. The same ribose and ribosylation issues remain.
suggestions: Use the direct cTMP ATP branch only as a lower-confidence alternative, not as a primary route.
Add Ni(II), wet-dry cycles, and realistic product distribution details to the cTMP phosphorylation step.
Consider generating AMP/ADP first, then ATP via acetyl phosphate and Fe3+.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is a compact route with a strong adenine source from formamide, but it is still undermined by weak nucleoside assembly and an only moderately supported direct ATP phosphorylation step. It is plausible as a parallel route but not a high-confidence main pathway.
suggestions: Retain the formamide branch but connect it to a more selective adenosine-forming route.
Demote direct adenosine-to-ATP phosphorylation relative to the ADP-to-ATP route.
Include the specific surface mineral/cation context required for cTMP chemistry.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway scores well on environmental storytelling because hydrothermal formaldehyde feeds surface sugar chemistry before joining the nucleotide assembly branch. That interconnection matches the review's proposed multi-environment scenario. However, the chemical bottlenecks are unchanged: ribose realism, direct adenosine formation, and partially underspecified phosphorylation chemistry.
suggestions: Keep this as an inter-environment scaffold but revise the sugar-to-nucleoside transition.
Acknowledge that hydrothermal formaldehyde production is supported, while downstream ribose accumulation is much less certain.
Add more explicit transport/concentration rationale between settings.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway excels in highlighting a key literature theme: hydrothermal proto-energetics merging with surface nucleotide assembly and ending in ATP. The weakest element is exactly that merger molecule, acetyl phosphate, because the proposed hydrothermal synthesis from formate + orthophosphate is not well established. The ATP-forming endpoint itself is excellent.
suggestions: Rework acetyl phosphate production through a more plausible vent-compatible route or label it explicitly speculative.
Preserve the environmental merger concept because it aligns well with current theory.
Support adenosine formation with a selective literature route.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The sugar-fragment branch is one of the best-supported parts of the network and aligns strongly with Sutherland-type chemistry. However, in this ATP network it is not used to generate adenosine through an experimentally validated purine assembly route; instead it converges back onto direct adenine + ribose glycosylation. Thus a strong subnetwork is connected to a weak bridge.
suggestions: Exploit the aminooxazole/ribo-aminooxazoline chemistry in a way that leads to validated nucleotide scaffolds rather than only as a side branch.
If the branch is intended as supporting evidence for sugar-fragment availability, say so explicitly and lower pathway confidence.
Integrate modern purine-selective scaffold chemistry for a stronger adenosine connection.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The adenine convergence is a good network feature because both HCN and formamide routes are literature-supported. But the pathway still narrows through the least convincing step, direct adenosine formation, before ending in a lower-confidence one-pot cTMP ATP step. Good as a redundancy branch, not as a principal synthesis route.
suggestions: Keep the adenine convergence but redirect it to a stronger nucleoside assembly route.
Treat one-pot ATP phosphorylation as auxiliary, not definitive.
Explicitly distinguish high-confidence from speculative reactions.

evaluation.network_feedback: The network is strong topologically and conceptually, and it aligns well with the current literature's central conclusion that ATP prebiotic synthesis is best treated as a multi-environment integration problem rather than a single continuous one-pot sequence. It has ample node count, clear hubs, multiple convergence points, and no obvious isolated subgraphs. Environmental interconnection is a major strength: hydrothermal CO2/H2 reduction feeds surface carbon chemistry, surface chemistry builds adenine and sugar/nucleoside precursors, and the biochemical stage captures the best-supported terminal ATP-forming step. Literature alignment is good overall for adenine synthesis via DAMN/AICN, formamide-to-adenine chemistry, sugar fragment formation from HCN photoredox chemistry, 2-aminooxazole/ribo-aminooxazoline chemistry, DAP phosphorylation, and especially ADP-to-ATP phosphorylation by acetyl phosphate with Fe3+. However, the network is not approvable at the >=0.9 level because several key bridging reactions are much weaker than presented: free-ribose production from formose chemistry is now considered highly problematic under mild conditions; direct adenine + ribose condensation to adenosine is not the strongest literature route and undercuts target coherence; hydrothermal acetyl phosphate generation from formate + phosphate is insufficiently established; and cTMP phosphorylation chemistry is somewhat oversimplified relative to the reported Ni(II)/wet-dry context and mixed product distribution. The network is therefore a good integrative hypothesis map, but not yet a high-confidence chemically coherent ATP synthesis network.
